summary: Descriptive overview of nuisance in substantive law: private vs public, balancing test, remedies.
key_points: private vs public is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for nuisance are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine nuisance in litigation.
The doctrine of nuisance has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding nuisance requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
